expectations.text: Recommends against directly injecting the public IFormSubmissionAppService across module boundaries
expectations.evidence: Opens with: "Short answer: no ŌĆö don't inject `IFormSubmissionAppService` into `ReportAppService`." and explains it "couples one module's internal API to another and turns a normal client-facing app service into an unintended cross-module contract." Reinforced in the TL;DR.

expectations.text: Recommends ABP Integration Services as the right pattern, explicitly mentioning the [IntegrationService] attribute
expectations.evidence: "The right pattern in ABP is an **Integration Service**" and shows `[IntegrationService]` attribute on the interface; also references `Volo.Abp.Application.Services.IntegrationServiceAttribute` in the differences table.

expectations.text: Defines the integration-service interface in the producing module's *.Application.Contracts (or *.Contracts) project
expectations.evidence: File path comment: `// Acme.Forms.Application.Contracts/IntegrationServices/IFormSubmissionIntegrationService.cs` and explicitly says "Contract goes in `Acme.Forms.Application.Contracts` (so consumers can reference just the contract assembly)."

expectations.text: Shows the implementation in the producing module's *.Application project (or equivalent), inheriting ApplicationService and implementing the [IntegrationService] interface
expectations.evidence: File path `// Acme.Forms.Application/IntegrationServices/FormSubmissionIntegrationService.cs` with class `FormSubmissionIntegrationService : ApplicationService, IFormSubmissionIntegrationService`.

expectations.text: Notes that integration services are hidden from the public API surface (and/or audit-off by default / not [Authorize] by default)
expectations.evidence: Comment on the interface: "hidden from /api, audit-off by default, no [Authorize] by default" and table rows show "Visible in default `/api` host: No", "Audit logging: Off by default", "[Authorize] expected: Typically not".

expectations.text: Explains the in-process modular-monolith vs microservice transport difference (DI in-process now vs HTTP / generated proxy across services), framed as 'same contract, different transport'
expectations.evidence: Section "In-process now, microservice later ŌĆö same contract": "ABP's DI just resolves `IFormSubmissionIntegrationService` to the in-process `FormSubmissionIntegrationService` ŌĆö no HTTP hop, no serialization" and "If you later split Forms and Reports into separate microservices, the `[IntegrationService]` is exposed over `/integration-api/...` behind the API gateway, and Reports consumes it via a generated proxy client. Same interface, same DI shape; only the transport changes."

expectations.text: Specifies that the consumer module's AbpModule depends only on the producer's *.Application.Contracts module (e.g. [DependsOn(typeof(AcmeFormsApplicationContractsModule))] on the Reports.Application module) ŌĆö NOT on the producer's full *.Application module. This contracts-only dependency is the load-bearing detail that preserves the module boundary
expectations.evidence: The response addresses csproj-level project references only ŌĆö line 71: "the consuming side just needs a project reference (or NuGet dep) on **`Acme.Forms.Application.Contracts`** ... Don't reference `Acme.Forms.Application` from Reports; the contract is enough." It never mentions `[DependsOn]`, `AbpModule`, or `AcmeFormsApplicationContractsModule`. Per the strict reading, mentioning project references / the contracts assembly is not enough ŌĆö the contracts-only `[DependsOn(typeof(AcmeFormsApplicationContractsModule))]` attribute on the consumer's `AcmeReportsApplicationModule` is the load-bearing wiring detail and it is absent from the response.

claims.claim: Integration services are exposed at /integration-api/... only when explicitly enabled
claims.evidence: Aligns with ABP 10.3 docs ŌĆö integration services use a separate route convention and are hidden by default unless ExposeIntegrationServices is enabled.
Matches ABP framework option name for controlling integration-service auto-controller exposure in the host.

claims.claim: Don't hand-write a controller for the integration service
claims.evidence: Consistent with ABP guidance ŌĆö integration-service exposure is wired via the integration-services pipeline rather than explicit controllers.

claims.claim: Consumer should reference Acme.Forms.Application.Contracts and not Acme.Forms.Application
claims.evidence: The response correctly states this at the project-reference level, but it stops short of the AbpModule [DependsOn] wiring that the tightened 7th assertion now requires.

suggestions.assertion: Specifies that the consumer module's AbpModule depends only on the producer's *.Application.Contracts module ...
suggestions.reason: The tightened wording is good and discriminating ŌĆö it correctly distinguishes csproj-level project references from AbpModule [DependsOn] wiring, and catches a real gap in this response. Worth keeping the parenthetical example `[DependsOn(typeof(AcmeFormsApplicationContractsModule))]` so future graders apply the same strict bar.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are discriminating and well-aligned with ABP's prescribed pattern; the tightened 7th assertion exposes a substantive omission that the looser version missed.
